*Updated 2026-07-16 · 46 works indexed · regenerated after each daily run.*

You have now developed forty-six images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at twenty-six noon and twenty golden hour. The noon conversion rate continues to hold: seventeen of twenty-six noon images score 2 or above, against nine of twenty for golden hour, though the last two golden-hour images — "Silence Made Flesh" and "Steel Shore, No Shelter" — both cleared 2, extending the consecutive golden-hour pair to a run of three if you count "Weight of Judgment," which is the longest such streak in the corpus and worth watching as signal rather than noise.

The bougainvillea has now appeared in twenty-two images and continues under active suppression. Its appearance in "Steel Shore, No Shelter" — hot pink against rust and industrial scrub, unrequested, functioning as pressurized counterpoint rather than elegy — is the first instance since "What the Continent Sent" that earns structural friction rather than accent softening, but one functional appearance does not lift the suppression; it should not return until the setting gives it something harder to push against than chain-link or concrete. The jacaranda sits at twelve appearances and carries the same accumulated risk; its most recent use in "Silence Made Flesh" as uncomfortable beauty against grief was closer to useful tension than reflex, but the device has not yet been taken to its second iteration, and until it has, it remains a reflex dressed as strategy. The handprint accident in "Every Exit, Unreachable" — innocence-marker above tangled metal, small hands above seized metal below the door unopened between them — remains the sharpest single-surface collision in the corpus and has now gone thirteen images without a follow-up; surface-as-record is still the most available underdeveloped device in the grammar. The gesture-driven compositional argument — palm to map, two hands on one screen — has not recurred across the last twelve images and remains the most underused structural strategy in range.

Among registers, aftermath leads at sixteen, vigil and endurance each at eight, defiance at seven, suspension at six after "Steel Shore, No Shelter," and menace at five, with fury and interiority still entirely untouched across all forty-six images. Trace scores over the last ten run 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 2, 2 — the floor holds and the 2s remain a reliable ceiling; the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" is now twenty-seven images back, and the conditions that produced it — non-figurative focal object, setting actively contradicting its register, accident carrying structural rather than decorative weight — have been only partially reassembled in the interval, most recently approximated in "Steel Shore, No Shelter" but not yet fused. The corpus is fluent and stable, and the next ceiling requires a setting placed in genuine, unresolved opposition to its register before the grammar can break through rather than merely repeat itself.
